scaled_oracle_cases.description: Parse input with two tumors and alphabetic placeholders in C and p* columns.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify the parser correctly handles two tumors and converts alphabetic placeholders to None.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When mu has three values (normal, tumor1, tumor2), C contains colon-separated parts with comma-separated values where some are letters, and p* contains colon-separated parts with comma-separated values where some are letters, the parser must return mu_tumors as list of two floats, C as list of two lists with None for letters, and p* as list of two lists with None for letters.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A parser that only handles single tumor will mis-parse C and p*; a parser that tries to int() or float() letters will raise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.3,0.3\tA,4:3,5\t0.1,X:0.2,0.4\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Increase data complexity by adding a second tumor to the input line, requiring the parser to handle multi-tumor formatting (transposed copy numbers and probabilities).
Introduce alphabetic placeholders in the C and p* columns to test robust handling of non-numeric values (None conversion).
